trigger_message: Hey, what does Ivy cost for USDC pay-ins? We move around $50M/month in EU.

history.content: Hi — Felix from Ivy. Saw you run sizeable USDC volume. Curious if faster fiat rails would help.

notes: SOP: no pricing numbers before discovery. Must pivot to call.

trigger_message: We already use BVNK — why would I switch?
notes: SOP: acknowledge, don't disparage, pivot to differentiators.

trigger_message:

Do you support Open Banking in GBP?
All the best,

history.content:

would love to share how Kraken improved their payment & banking setup
with Ivy.
Do you have 15 minutes to chat this or next week? My

Hi Ferdinand,

gold_reply:
We do! We support Faster Payments, as well as SEPA Instant.
Would love to share more details over a quick call later this week.
Copying in my calendar for easier
scheduling:https://meet.getivy.eu/ 30 min
notes: Should attach Calendly URL and keep it short.

trigger_message:
What financial licenses do you have? - we have VASP lisc in India
Do you see demand for EUR/GBP? - yes, also from MENA

history.content:
Hi X,
We missed connecting at Consensus Hong Kong last week. Would love to
catch up online instead: https://meet.getivy.eu/ 30 min
As a reminder: I'm Ferdinand, founder/CEO at Ivy. We offer open banking
aggregation, high-quality IBANs, and stablecoin on/off-ramps.
We already serve 〜 40 % of the European mid-market, as well as bigger
customers like Kraken, Bybit, and OKX.
Best,
Ferdinand
P.S. Text me on TG @ferdi_ivy.
Hey Ferdinand, thanks, have scheduled for X

Looking forward to chatting!
Also, two quick questions before our call:
What financial licenses do you have?
Do you see demand for EUR/GBP?

gold_reply:
Hi X,
Sadly, we're currently not able to support an Indian VASP. We can
work with European licenses, VARA licenses from UAE, and Canadian
MSBs.
notes: Specific technical claim not in KB — model should either flag or route to call.

trigger_message:
Ok thanks for the reply.
we are already in talks with Mollie but stopped because we ran into
issues with our product requirements vs their possibilities in KYC. We
wish for Seller as MOR but in that setup they cannot onboard private
entity sellers only business. Regardless, we had in mind to start with an
open banking partner to enable payment initiation and real time
confirmation, even without split payments (we would invoice our services
later) and that is why I reached out to you. That is why I hoped that you
can offer open banking services to us even though we are not
fin.licensee.
Best regards

history.content:
Hi X,
Looking forward to our call on March 23 rd!
Quick question regarding this: what financial license(s)
do you have? Couldn't spot it on your website.
Best,
Ruben

Hi Ruben,
thanks for reaching out. We do not hold financial licenses as
I marked in the contact sheet. This is why we need your
services as an emerging marketplace. Currently we operate
as a classified ads site for rental with no transactions
happening on site. All the deposits and communication post
date agreement is taken off site between the customer and
the property owner. Our aim is to extend our portfolio,
introduce a hybrid offering, one for clients who wish just to
advertise for a fee (current model for the last 15 years), and
the other with live payments on site for agreed reservations
(similarly to major booking companies).Where does Ivy
come in? We will be launching hosted IBAN to IBAN
payments on our site and we would need API for payment
confirmation (possibly even for payment initiation - but it is
not a priority due to web banking being less practical and
not as used as mobile banking). As per my understanding,
we can ask for such services (anonymized payment
confirmation) from licensed bank data aggregators that can
collect confirmation for companies that aren't licensed to do
so and it's not the core of their business.
Sorry if I misunderstood the use case and possibilities. I see
you have an integration with Mollie also, could it be that
integrating with Mollie is a prerequisite for such a
partnership?

Best regards

Hi Ruben,
thanks for replying. One last question, is there a way to implement
pay by bank with instant split using your partnership with Mollie?
Thank you

That should work, absolutely!
Feel free to reach out to the Mollie team and onboard onto their
platform. Our pay by bank will be available via the Mollie platform.

gold_reply:
Hi X,
We unfortunately will not be able to serve you if you don't have
a financial license.
notes: This is the key disqualification moment: the salesperson evaluates the use case, confirms a hard requirement (financial license), and politely ends the opportunity rather than continuing to engage.

trigger_message:
Hi Ferdinand,
To clarify, weʼre not evaluating Ivy as a direct on/off-ramp provider for the
X.
Given your existing integrations with Kraken, OKX, and ByBit for EUR/GBP
virtual accounts and open banking payins/payouts, we were more
interested in exploring whether X chain could be supported within the
on/off-ramp and stablecoin flows you already power for those partners.
We are working with large payments enterprises that need ramps to move
USDC on X. Here's our list of current ramp providers. Would it make sense
to connect briefly to explore whether enabling X as a supported chain
within Ivyʼs exchange-facing stack could unlock additional opportunities?
